# 🚀 To-Do API — Multi-Container (API + DB) with Automated CI/CD & Quality Gates

![CI/CD](https://img.shields.io/badge/CI%2FCD-GitHub%20Actions-2088FF?logo=githubactions&logoColor=white)
![Testing](https://img.shields.io/badge/Tested%20With-Jest%20%2F%20Supertest-C21325?logo=jest&logoColor=white)
![Docker](https://img.shields.io/badge/Container-Docker%20%7C%20Compose-2496ED?logo=docker&logoColor=white)
![Database](https://img.shields.io/badge/Database-PostgreSQL-4169E1?logo=postgresql&logoColor=white)
![AWS](https://img.shields.io/badge/Cloud-AWS%20EC2-FF9900?logo=amazonaws&logoColor=white)
![Node.js](https://img.shields.io/badge/Runtime-Node.js-339933?logo=nodedotjs&logoColor=white)

A production-ready, multi-container REST API for managing tasks. This repository tracks an end-to-end **DevOps progression** demonstrating container orchestration, persistent databases, automated testing suites, and strict **CI/CD quality gates** deploying to **AWS Cloud**.

> **Day 1:** Stateless App + Docker + GitHub Actions + AWS EC2  
> **Day 2:** Multi-Container Setup + PostgreSQL + Docker Compose + Volumes & Networking  
> **Day 3:** Modular Architecture + Automated Test Suite (10 Endpoints) + CI/CD Quality Gates (`test` → `deploy`)

---

## 📐 Architecture & CI/CD Pipeline

  <text x="550" y="65" text-anchor="middle" font-size="13" fill="#718096" font-weight="500" style="white-space: pre;">Push to main → Automated Test → Automated Deploy → Live on AWS</text>

    <rect x="30" y="150" width="160" height="110" rx="8" fill="#ebf8ff" stroke="#3182ce" stroke-width="2"/>

  <line x1="190" y1="205" x2="230" y2="205" stroke="#4a5568" stroke-width="2" marker-end="url(#arrow)"/>
  <text x="210" y="195" text-anchor="middle" font-size="11" fill="#718096" style="white-space: pre;">triggers</text>
  <g filter="url(#drop-shadow)" style="">
    <rect x="240" y="110" width="310" height="260" rx="10" fill="#f0fff4" stroke="#38a169" stroke-width="2" style=""/>
    <text x="395" y="135" text-anchor="middle" font-size="15" font-weight="700" fill="#22543d" style="white-space: pre;">GitHub Actions Workflow</text>
    <g style="">
      <rect x="255" y="155" width="280" height="75" rx="6" fill="#ffffff" stroke="#cbd5e0" stroke-width="1.5" style=""/>
      <text x="270" y="176" font-size="13" font-weight="bold" fill="#2d3748" style="white-space: pre;">Job 1: test</text>
      <text x="270" y="195" font-size="11" fill="#4a5568" style="white-space: pre;">• Spin up temporary DB container</text>
      <text x="270" y="212" font-size="11" fill="#4a5568" style="white-space: pre;">• Run 10 automated tests ("api.test.js")</text>
      <text x="463" y="176" font-size="12" font-weight="bold" fill="#38a169" style="white-space: pre; font-size: 12px;">Status: ✅</text>
    </g>

    <text x="405" y="244" font-size="10" font-weight="bold" fill="#38a169" style="white-space: pre;">If tests pass (needs: test)</text>

      <rect x="255" y="265" width="280" height="85" rx="6" fill="#ffffff" stroke="#cbd5e0" stroke-width="1.5"/>
      <text x="270" y="286" font-size="13" font-weight="bold" fill="#2d3748" style="white-space: pre;">Job 2: deploy</text>

      <text x="270" y="334" font-size="11" fill="#4a5568" style="white-space: pre;">3. Run production deploy script</text>
    </g>
  </g>

Every `git push` triggers a multi-job quality gate pipeline. Broken code is automatically caught in GitHub's temporary testing environment and blocked from reaching the live AWS EC2 instance.

<svg viewBox="0 0 1000 480" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" font-family="Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif">
  <defs>
    <marker id="arrow" markerWidth="10" markerHeight="10" refX="8" refY="3" orient="auto" markerUnits="strokeWidth">
      <path d="M0,0 L0,6 L9,3 z" fill="#4a5568"/>
    </marker>
  </defs>

  <rect x="0" y="0" width="1000" height="480" fill="#ffffff"/>

  <text x="500" y="35" text-anchor="middle" font-size="22" font-weight="700" fill="#1a202c">To-Do API — CI/CD Pipeline with Quality Gate Architecture</text>
  <text x="500" y="58" text-anchor="middle" font-size="13" fill="#718096">Push to main → Automated Test Suite → Safe Automated Deployment → Live on AWS</text>

  <g>
    <rect x="15" y="150" width="120" height="100" rx="10" fill="#ebf8ff" stroke="#3182ce" stroke-width="2"/>
    <text x="75" y="190" text-anchor="middle" font-size="30">💻</text>

    <text x="330" y="115" text-anchor="middle" font-size="14" font-weight="700" fill="#22543d">GitHub Actions Workflows</text>
    
    <rect x="195" y="135" width="270" height="75" rx="6" fill="#ffffff" stroke="#cbd5e0" stroke-width="1.5"/>
    <text x="210" y="158" font-size="12" font-weight="bold" fill="#2d3748">🧪 Job 1: Test (Quality Gate)</text>
    <text x="210" y="175" font-size="11" fill="#718096">• Spins up Ephemeral DB Container</text>
    <text x="210" y="192" font-size="11" fill="#38a169">• Runs 10 API Integration Tests ✅</text>

    <line x1="330" y1="210" x2="330" y2="225" stroke="#38a169" stroke-width="2" marker-end="url(#arrow)"/>

    <rect x="195" y="233" width="270" height="75" rx="6" fill="#ffffff" stroke="#cbd5e0" stroke-width="1.5"/>
    <text x="210" y="255" font-size="12" font-weight="bold" fill="#2d3748">🚀 Job 2: Deploy (needs: test)</text>
    <text x="210" y="272" font-size="11" fill="#718096">• Runs ONLY if tests pass</text>
    <text x="210" y="289" font-size="11" fill="#3182ce">• SSH into AWS EC2 Instance</text>
  </g>

  <line x1="485" y1="200" x2="520" y2="200" stroke="#4a5568" stroke-width="2" marker-end="url(#arrow)"/>
  <text x="502" y="190" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#718096">SSH</text>

  <g>
    <rect x="530" y="90" width="455" height="260" rx="10" fill="#fffaf0" stroke="#dd6b20" stroke-width="2"/>
    <text x="757" y="115" text-anchor="middle" font-size="15" font-weight="700" fill="#7b341e">AWS EC2 Production Engine</text>

    <rect x="545" y="140" width="425" height="195" rx="8" fill="#f7fafc" stroke="#4a5568" stroke-width="1.5" stroke-dasharray="6,4"/>
    <text x="560" y="160" font-size="11" font-weight="bold" fill="#4a5568">🔒 Isolated Private Docker Bridge Network</text>

    <rect x="560" y="180" width="170" height="135" rx="6" fill="#ebf8ff" stroke="#3182ce" stroke-width="2"/>
    <text x="645" y="215" text-anchor="middle" font-size="24">🐳</text>
    <text x="645" y="240" text-anchor="middle" font-size="12" font-weight="bold" fill="#2c5282">Node.js API</text>
    <text x="645" y="258" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#4a5568">No Dev Tools (--omit=dev)</text>
    <text x="645" y="275" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#4a5568">Port 3000 Exposed</text>
    <text x="645" y="300" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#e53e3e">depends_on: db (healthy)</text>

    <line x1="730" y1="245" x2="780" y2="245" stroke="#2b6cb0" stroke-width="2" stroke-dasharray="2,2"/>
    
    <rect x="785" y="180" width="170" height="135" rx="6" fill="#ebf4ff" stroke="#4269e1" stroke-width="2"/>

    <text x="870" y="240" text-anchor="middle" font-size="12" font-weight="bold" fill="#1a365d">PostgreSQL DB</text>
    <text x="870" y="258" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#4a5568">Private Port 5432</text>
    <text x="870" y="285" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#2b6cb0">📁 Named Volume Mount</text>
  </g>

  <line x1="645" y1="410" x2="645" y2="352" stroke="#4a5568" stroke-width="2" marker-end="url(#arrow)"/>
  <text x="705" y="380" text-anchor="middle" font-size="11" fill="#718096">HTTP Request (Port 3000)</text>

  <g>
    <rect x="555" y="410" width="180" height="50" rx="8" fill="#faf5ff" stroke="#805ad5" stroke-width="2"/>
    <text x="645" y="440" text-anchor="middle" font-size="13" font-weight="600" fill="#44337a">🌐 Public Internet Clients</text>
  </g>
</svg>

---

## 🧰 Tech Stack

| Layer | Technology | Key Implementation Details |
|---|---|---|
| **Application Runtime** | Node.js / Express.js | Modularized (`app.js` routes vs `index.js` server bootstrap) |
| **Testing Framework** | Jest / Supertest | Automated integration tests running in ephemeral environments |
| **Database Container** | PostgreSQL | Multi-container link, custom init schemas |
| **Containerization** | Docker & Docker Compose | Multi-stage optimization, healthcheck synchronization |
| **CI/CD Automation** | GitHub Actions | **Quality Gates:** Two-stage dependency workflow (`test` → `deploy`) |
| **Cloud Infrastructure** | AWS EC2 | Custom security groups, network path constraints |
| **Data Persistence** | Docker Named Volumes | Decoupled local file structure mappings |

---

## ✨ Key Features & Architectural Highlights

- ✅ **Automated Quality Gate (NEW):** The `.github/workflows/deploy.yml` enforces automated pre-deploy checks. Production deployment only executes if the comprehensive test suite passes inside GitHub Actions.
- ✅ **Decoupled Application Structure (NEW):** Split API routing logic (`app.js`) from server initialization (`index.js`). This enables headless API integration testing without binding live ports.
- ✅ **Lean Production Optimization:** Production Docker images specifically exclude development dependencies via `--omit=dev`. Testing frameworks exist *only* inside CI pipelines, reducing production vulnerability attack surfaces.
- ✅ **Multi-Container Orchestration:** Unified provisioning of API server and database container via `docker-compose.yml`.
- ✅ **State Persistence:** Persistent database storage using **Docker Named Volumes** — data remains intact across rebuilds and deployments.
- ✅ **Network Isolation:** Built-in bridge network prevents public external lookups directly hitting the database engine port (`5432`). Only the API container can connect.
- ✅ **Race-Condition Prevention:** Uses explicit container startup dependencies combined with database readiness health checks (`pg_isready`).

---

## 🧪 Automated Testing Suite

The repository contains **10 end-to-end integration tests** (`tests/api.test.js`) testing all application layers:

* Ensure `/health` endpoint correctly reports database status.
* Validate full task lifecycle: **Create**, **List**, **Complete**, and **Delete**.
* Enforce strict input verification (e.g., rejecting empty task names).
* Ensure proper HTTP status codes (`400 Bad Request`, `404 Not Found`) are returned for invalid IDs or malformed payloads.

### How Verification Works
The pipeline workflow spins up an isolated, temporary PostgreSQL database container inside GitHub Actions, runs all 10 tests against `app.js`, and tears the test environment down automatically upon completion. 

> **Note on Verification:** Tests run strictly within the GitHub Actions runner. They will fail if executed directly inside the production EC2 container because testing frameworks and test runners are deliberately stripped out of the live production image for security and performance optimizations.

---

## 📡 API Endpoints

| Method | Endpoint | Description |
|--------|-----------|-------------|
| `GET` | `/health` | Core system & DB link availability checks |
| `GET` | `/tasks` | Retrieves all relational database task listings |
| `POST` | `/tasks` | Appends a single structured entry to the DB |
| `PATCH` | `/tasks/:id/complete` | Updates completeness fields |
| `DELETE` | `/tasks/:id` | Drop specified entry id from table |

---

## 🖥️ Local Execution & Development

### Prerequisites
Ensure both **Docker** and **Docker Compose** are installed on your local workstation.

### 1. Configure Environment Variables
Create a `.env` file in the root workspace directory:
```env
PORT=3000
DB_HOST=db
DB_PORT=5432
DB_USER=postgres
DB_PASSWORD=your_secure_password
DB_NAME=todo_db
